flashcards.front: What is the difference between geometric and point operations?
flashcards.back: Geometric operations change pixel positions, while point operations change pixel values without altering positions.
An affine transformation preserves points, straight lines, and planes. It includes translation, rotation, scaling, and shearing.
Homogeneous coordinates represent 2D points as 3D vectors (x,y,w), allowing geometric transformations to be represented as matrix multiplications.

flashcards.front: Name three interpolation methods used in geometric transformations
flashcards.back: Nearest neighbor, bilinear interpolation, and bicubic interpolation.
Image registration is the process of aligning two or more images of the same scene taken at different times or from different viewpoints.
